summary: Final Beta culture/history/language writer review is ready for qa_reviewer. I added an independent culture-review evidence file that accepts the current M7 scenario/person/event/tutorial/hint/encyclopedia/audio/art/localization set only as Beta review evidence, not formal content lock. The evidence records accepted, request-change-before-lock, blocked-for-production, and Human Gate-required findings with exact paths, source records, claim records, uncertainty labels, and rationale. It incorporates product-owner directive M7-UI-LOCALIZATION-ART-COORDINATION-001 and the newly returned visual handoff as future UI/localization/visual quality-track constraints. The art/UX handoff is now present and schema-valid, but remains a dependency pending systems/QA review, not a substitute for culture review.

tests_run.result: Final observed status includes my modified project/tasks/active/M7-BETA-CULTURE-REVIEW-001.json, untracked content-source/m7-beta-culture-review/, and this writer handoff. Concurrent allowed-path orchestration/visual-thread changes are also present: project/goal-mode-state.json, project/messages/outbox/GOAL-MODE-CONTINUATION.json, project/model-routing-state.json, project/tasks/active/M7-VISUAL-UX-HANDOFF-001.json, project/tasks/thread-registry.json, the returned M7-VISUAL-UX-HANDOFF visual files, and the route to systems review. docs/M7_UI_Localization_Art_Handoff_Prompts.md was not touched.
VALID project/tasks/active/M7-BETA-CULTURE-REVIEW-001.json after moving task status to REVIEW.

Task list shows M7-BETA-CULTURE-REVIEW-001 in REVIEW. At the time of taskctl list it showed M7-VISUAL-UX-HANDOFF-001 in IN_PROGRESS; a concurrent route later advanced that task file to REVIEW for systems_architect review. Upstream M7 historical/cultural/language, scenario/person/event, tutorial/hints/encyclopedia, and audio/art/localization tasks are CLOSED.
M7-SAVE-MIGRATION-CLOUD-BOUNDARIES-001 is the only ready task while Beta culture review is in REVIEW.

tests_run.command: node -e JSON parse/shape check for M7-BETA-CULTURE-REVIEW-001 and content-source/m7-beta-culture-review/beta-culture-review-evidence.json
tests_run.result: JSON shape ok: task REVIEW; evidence has accepted/request-change/blocked/Human Gate findings; route qa_reviewer; notContentLockAcceptance true; DEFER_MANUAL_NODE_BATTLE preserved; product-owner directive path cited.
VALID visual_design_reviewer handoff envelope. This confirms the art/UX handoff arrived, but it remains pending systems_architect and qa_reviewer review.
Content validation passed for m1, m2, m3, m6 map, m6 scenario, and m7 beta scenario/person/event set; M7 manifest hash remains 92aed91e.

decisions.reason: The M7 historical/cultural/language baseline has source policy, 8 claim records, review gates, bias checks, zero accepted Beta real-world claims, and explicit Human Gate routing.

The scenario/person/event set has 6 claim records, 3 scenarios, 6 persons, 3 titles, 5 events, 36 localization records, explicit RESEARCH REQUIRED/LANGUAGE_REVIEW_REQUIRED/CULTURE_HUMAN_GATE_REQUIRED states, and no fixed culture buff or formal lock acceptance.
Tutorial, hint, and encyclopedia surfaces are accepted as generated/QA-checked projections from accepted content-source records; they must keep uncertainty labels and Human Gate language visible.

Audio/art/localization coverage uses static neutral slots and explicitly disclaims period instrument, sacred sound, flag, script, temple, manuscript, court-symbol, place, and culture-specific production claims.

Scenario chronology, participants, campaign-window claims, public-facing names/titles/transliterations, zh-Hans wording quality, and UI player-mode terminology need source/language review or must remain visibly review-blocked before lock.

Formal content lock, coercion/forced-movement reward framing, culture-specific production assets, identity shortcut exceptions, sacred/religious UI use, and manual node battle reversal remain Human Gates.

M7-UI-LOCALIZATION-ART-COORDINATION-001 and the returned M7-VISUAL-UX-HANDOFF visual thread output are cited as future quality-track constraints. The handoff is present but still requires systems/QA review; it is not a substitute for culture review and not authorization to implement UI/design tokens/i18n/app shell.

risks.summary: Formal content lock remains a future Human Gate; this REVIEW must not be read as ACCEPTED/CLOSED or production historical certainty.

Culture-specific art, music, scripts, symbols, sacred imagery, clothing, architecture, weapons, names, titles, and transliterations remain blocked from production acceptance without source-specific cultural review and Human Gate when major risk remains.

Forced movement / population-loss content is correctly a Human Gate review hook, but any future reward framing must keep victim groups, source regions, immediate costs, and long-term consequences explicit.
Academic candidate sources in current records are metadata-only or source-discovery-only where pageOrSection is RESEARCH REQUIRED.
risks.evidence: review evidence methodology fullTextLimit and REQUEST-CHANGE-BEFORE-LOCK-001.

risks.summary: M7-VISUAL-UX-HANDOFF-001 returned visual thread files during this review; later UI beautification must still wait for systems/QA review and must hide debug/prototype text from player mode.

blockers.summary: Formal acceptance of scenario years, chronology, participants, and named historical persons as verified facts is blocked until page/section evidence exists or records remain RESEARCH REQUIRED.
Culture-specific production visuals/audio/symbols and public-facing names/titles are blocked until historical/cultural/language review clears them.
Formal content lock acceptance and major cultural-risk decisions require Human Gate; writer did not decide them.

requested_action: Review content-source/m7-beta-culture-review/beta-culture-review-evidence.json and this writer REVIEW handoff. Verify that accepted items are only Beta evidence, request-change/blocked/Human Gate findings are precise enough for content lock, M7-UI-LOCALIZATION-ART-COORDINATION-001 is correctly treated as a future UI/localization/visual dependency, and no product code or forbidden scope was changed.
